dependencies: Reliance on independent subhaulers for vehicle transportation in U.S., Canada, Brazil, Republic of Ireland, Germany, Finland, U.A.E., Oman, Bahrain, and Spain
Company-owned fleet for transportation in U.S., U.K., and Germany
Dependence on third-party vehicle transport companies
risks: Increase in fuel costs may lead to increased prices charged by independent subhaulers

regulatory_risks: Regulation, supervision and licensing under various international, federal, provincial, state, and local statutes, ordinances and regulations
Vehicle acquisition and sales regulated by various state, provincial and foreign motor vehicle departments
Local zoning requirements for location of storage facilities
Environmental laws and regulations regarding protection of the environment
Potential for enhanced regulation due to increased focus on climate change and environmental matters
Changes in laws or interpretation of laws affecting import and export of vehicles
Imposition of import duties by foreign countries
Compliance with anti-bribery laws (U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act, U.K. Bribery Act, Brazil Clean Companies Act, India's Prevention of Corruption Act)
Data privacy regulations (GDPR in EU, CCPA in California, LGPD in Brazil)
Vehicle titling, sales, and registration rules and procedures
Exporting regulations and reporting requirements to agencies and law enforcement
Potential violations of money laundering laws under investigation by U.S. Department of Justice

sanction_risks: Compliance with trade controls and economic sanctions
Potential impact from military conflicts including Russian invasion of Ukraine and recent events in the Middle East
Tariffs, trade barriers, trade disputes affecting international operations
